kale-and-brussels-slaw-with-quinoa.name: Kale And Brussels Slaw With Quinoa
instructions: In a food processor, combine the shallots, vinegar, lemon juice, maple syrup, olive oil, salt and pepper and blend until smooth. Set aside.
Line a small baking sheet with Reynolds Wrap® Aluminum Foil. Set aside.

In a non-stick pan over medium heat, combine the hazelnuts, sugar and cayenne. Stir with a silicone spatula until the sugar has melted and sticks to the nuts, about 5 minutes; turn down the heat to prevent burning if needed. Spread the sticky nuts out on the foil-lined sheet to cool. When the nuts are cool enough to handle, break them up by hand.

Stem the kale and chop well. Using a mandoline, shred the Brussels sprouts, discarding the tough ends. Combine both in a large salad bowl. Add the hazelnuts, quinoa, pomegranate seeds, green onions and cheese. Dress to your liking and serve immediately.

kale-and-pink-grapefruit-salad.name: Kale And Pink Grapefruit Salad

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper

instructions: Using a sharp knife, cut peel and white pith from grapefruit; discard. Working over a small bowl, cut between membranes to release segments into bowl. Squeeze juice from membranes into another small bowl; add any accumulated juices from bowl with segments (there should be about 1/4 cup juice total). Whisk oil into juice and season to taste with salt and pepper.

Place kale in a large bowl and drizzle 3 Tbsp. dressing over. Toss to combine and let stand for 10 minutes while kale wilts slightly. Toss once more, then arrange grapefruit segments and avocado slices over kale. Drizzle with remaining dressing and serve.

kale-with-pomegranate-dressing-and-ricot.name: Kale With Pomegranate Dressing And Ricotta Salata

ingredients.item: Kosher salt and freshly ground black pepper
Red russian or purple kale or 2 bunches tuscan kale
ingredients.prep: ribs and stems removed, leaves torn into 2” pieces

instructions: Combine shallot, vinegar, and pomegranate molasses in a large bowl; season dressing with salt and pepper and let sit 5 minutes.
Add kale to dressing, season with salt and pepper, and massage dressing into leaves. Add pomegranate seeds and oil and toss to combine. Serve topped with ricotta salata.
DO AHEAD: Dressing can be made 4 hours ahead; cover and chill.

kefir-oats-nuts-and-maple-breakfast-jar.name: Kefir, Oats, Nuts, And Maple Breakfast Jar
instructions: Place kefir, oats, maple syrup, pecans, and almonds in two 8-oz jars or small bowls, dividing evenly; season lightly with salt. Cover and shake or stir to combine. Chill until oats have softened, 2–2 1/2 hours.

kensington.name: Kensington
instructions: Combine 2 oz. bourbon, ½ oz. dry vermouth, ½ oz. sweet vermouth, 2 dashes Peychaud’s bitters, and 1 bar spoon orange marmalade in a mixing glass with ice. Stir until well chilled, 20–25 seconds, then double-strain through a julep strainer and tea strainer into a coupe. Garnish with an orange twist.

kickin-pimento-cheese-spread.name: Kickin' Pimento Cheese Spread
instructions: Beat cream cheese, mayonnaise, and sour cream in a bowl with an electric mixer until smooth. Stir in Cheddar cheese, pimentos, cayenne pepper, and garlic powder. Cover and refrigerate until ready to serve.

king-trumpet-yakitori.name: King Trumpet Yakitori

ingredients.prep: trimmed, halved lengthwise, cut crosswise into 2-inch pieces

instructions: Soak scallion in ice water until crisp, at least 10 minutes. Drain.
Meanwhile, bring mirin, sake, soy sauce, and zarame sugar to a boil in a small saucepan; reduce heat and simmer until slightly reduced, 12–15 minutes. Set tare aside.

Prepare grill for medium heat. Thread 3 mushroom pieces onto each skewer. Brush lightly with oil and season lightly with salt. Grill until golden brown, about 1 minute per side. Then cook, brushing with tare and turning occasionally, until glazed and tender, about 2 minutes more. Serve mushrooms topped with scallions.
Do Ahead: Tare can be made 3 days ahead. Let cool; cover and chill. Reheat before using.

kneecaps.name: Kneecaps

ingredients.item: Sprinkles or colored sugar for decoration

instructions: Dissolve yeast in warm water in a small bowl. Let stand until yeast softens and begins to form a creamy foam, about 5 minutes.
Beat shortening, sugar, and salt together in a bowl. Beat in eggs, one at a time, while continuing to beat. Add milk and yeast mixture alternating with flour until smooth.
Place dough in a greased bowl and turn to coat. Cover with a light towel; let rise in a warm place until doubled in volume, 1 to 1 ½ hours.
Punch down dough and roll out on a floured surface to ½-inch thick. Cut into rounds with a 2-inch round cookie cutter, cover, and allow to rise 30 minutes more.
Heat oil in a deep fryer or large saucepan to 191°C (190 degrees C).

Press an indentation into center of each kneecap using your thumb. Lower kneecaps carefully into the hot oil a few at a time. Fry until golden brown, 1 to 2 minutes per side. Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate to drain; cool to room temperature, about 30 minutes. Repeat with remaining kneecaps.
Dust kneecaps with confectioners' sugar; fill indentations with whipped cream and sprinkle with colored sugar.

kokoretsi-in-crust-sheet-argiro.name: Kokoresti In Crust Sheet

ingredients.item: Pepper enough and salt with taste
Pepper enough and salt with taste

instructions: For the cokoreche on a crust Leaf in the ocean, heat the liver gently to cut it better.
Finely chop it with scissors and add it to a bowl along with the other injectors. This mixture removes 4 rolls of rooster on a crust sheet.
Lightly growth 3 crust leaves and place them on top of each other.
Place 1/3 of the filling along the leaves.
Leave a blank leave on the edges and fold into a folder and then roll.
Place the 4 rolls in a lightly oiled pyramidal parallelogram.
Lightly oil them up and sprinkle them with some water.
Place a little in the center so the steam runs away and they don't explore on the side, and make the rooster roll at 190°3′ for 45′-50′′.
It's better cut when it's lukewarm.
